module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats; setup/launch/sequencing-transition beats mood-exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Mood contract named verbatim in maintainer block: 'grounded competence — I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one' (line 85). Trace mood_score 8 at file scope; close 8. Prework's new 'Open a new Claude Code session in the repo you used for Module 1' (line 8) is a setup/launch beat — mood-exempt by carve-out.

Mood is grounded competence, not punitive. The Connections question names a recognisable rubber-stamp pattern ('we all do it') rather than shaming. Bring-or-scramble stakes line at line 12 is peer-flat — 'your call' returns agency rather than scolding.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with the discipline ('Reading a plan is finite. Push back with what you see; run a second-pass read for what you can't. Paired, they give a complete read'), naming the positive paired-read move. 'Approval inflation' appears only as a learning outcome to SPOT, not as the framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc line 304: 'M2 owns plan-mode-at-depth' and 'Pocock's grill-me skill remains forked... for M3 to reveal as authoring material.' The module inlines the walk-down as a plain prompt (line 36, exercise reference), not as a named skill. M3 first-skill-use beat protected. 'Next' (line 61) telegraphs M3 with 'packageable as a skill; you author your first one there' — forward hint, not pre-teach.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts (90%-correct / compounding / capture-your-world).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts (lines 50-57): bullet 3 names 'push-backs ship into memory' (compounding/capture-your-world); bullet 4 'Assume 90% on first pass' (90%-correct theme as bolded breadcrumb); bullet 5 names the pairing pattern (compounding via paired-reads). Breadcrumbs decorate survivor bullets, not all bullets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing function lives in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Push-back forcing function lives in the exercise's prompt fence (push-back-on-the-plan.md), not in module body. Save-the-rule prompt at line 46 ({{prompt:ae101-m2-integrate-branch}}) carries the 'one branch only if earned' forcing function in the prompt fence. Module body names the move, doesn't enact it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Connections paragraph (lines 28-30) names the room-harvest move; this is a deliberate exception named in maintainer block (line 89) — Connections-as-room-read carve-out, parallel to M1 and reaffirmed for future judges. No 'discuss with neighbour' / 'share with the room' / 'tell the room' cohort-management cues elsewhere in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opener lecture 'When a plan is good' (line 32) teaches plan-reading vocabulary — file-list / verification / assumptions — placed before the push-back exercise where the vocab earns itself. Closer lecture 'Where the rule could live' (line 38) is the meta-frame naming three downstream shapes (Slack triage / issue webhook / scheduled read) — properly placed AFTER both exercises.
M2 is not a contrast-mood module (mood is grounded competence, not the M5-style 'learn through doing, name it after' shape). Rule doesn't apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Named teaching moment: 'plan-mode approval inflation catches as the key teaching moment' (strategy doc line 236). Module reliably triggers it through the Connections question ('when was the last time Claude wrote a plan you approved without really reading?') + push-back exercise's forced two push-backs. P3 rubber-stamp escape hatch (maintainer block line 93) catches the failure mode if the persona tries to skip.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — hand-off to next module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closer lecture 'Where the rule could live' deliberately doesn't resolve — names three shapes for downstream automation without doing any of them. 'Next' paragraph (line 61) telegraphs M3 work without pre-teaching it. Grounded-competence mood stays grounded; doesn't flatten into 'we're done' or front-run M3's earn-the-trust mood.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module honors strategy-doc framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc framing: 'two reads (human + agent) catch what one doesn't' (line 296). Module's Big Idea (lines 3-4): 'Reading a plan is finite. Your own read catches some of it, a second agent walking the decision tree catches the rest... Paired, they give a complete read; neither alone does.' Verbatim fidelity in the paired-reads framing.
